content_en:
When the army returned to Al-Madinah after the war, some of the people of the city were not happy with their performance; tossing up earth in the air they shouted, “Look! The cowards who fled from the battlefield have come back!”

When the Prophet # saw this he said:

“Listen, these people are not cowards, neither have they run away from the battlefield. This is a military strategy - to refresh themselves and return to confront the enemy. ”

Doubtless this was a wise move by the Prophet 38.

To get a handful of companions out of the mouth of the dragon, as it were, is proof of great bravery and courage. This was the first martial exploit of Khalid bin Waleed 4 after he converted to Islam. As time passed he displayed his invaluable qualities of heroism and valour in ever greater measure.

The nightingale of the court of the Prophet %, Hassan bin Thabit &, recited extempore beautifully sad verses, which have become important classics of the elegiac genre of Arabic poetry. When he was martyred Ja’far bin Abi Talib - was about forty-one years old.

At the time of death he was reciting the verses of a war epic; the following is a rough translation -

This is great! Near now the pure Jannah and its cool waters;

For the Romans the torture of Jahannum is near.

These unbelievers, by birth are far from nobility;

It is my duty to lop off their heads as I pass them.

Emperor Najashi accepted Islam because he was moved by the invitation of Ja’far bin Abi Talib 4. ‘Najashi’ was a title common to all tulers of Ethiopia. The name of the ruler who was a contemporary of Ja’far bin Abi Talib 4 was Ashamah. It is written in the ‘Tabagat Ibn Sa‘d' that when the Prophet 3% returned to Al-Madinah after signing the Treaty of Hudaibiyah in the year 8 Hijrah, he sent ‘Amr bin Umayyah Al-Dhamri to Ethiopia to Emperor Najashi with a letter. He

received the letter and kissed it reverently, descending from his throne. Displaying respectful hospitality and humility, he sat down on the ground, and recited the oath of allegiance to Islam.

In the year 9'b Hijrah on his return from Tabook, he passed away. (To Allah we belong and to Him we return.)

In a tradition attributed to Jabir # and recorded in Sahih Al-Bukhari it is said that the Prophet # announced the passing away of Najashi to his Companions and offered his funeral prayers.

Ibn Habban has narrated on the authority of ‘Imran bin Hassain that the Prophet 3 said, “Your brother Najashi has expired, let us offer funeral prayers for him.” He moved forward and his Companions formed rows behind him. During the prayers he recited “Alla@ho Akbar’ four times. The swearing of the pledge of allegiance to Islam by Najashi in the presence of Ja’far bin Abi Talib .& is a historic incident.

The unique excellence of Ja’far bin Abi Talib & in appearance and character was indeed enviable. He cut off the legs of his horse before he was martyred and fought as if obsessed by his passion for martyrdom. This was the forerunner of great honours to be bestowed on him by Allah ¥%.

The fact that he was elevated to a lofty position in Paradise is proof and acknowledgement of the great services rendered by him to Islam. His

appointment as Commander-in-Chief in the Battle of Motah by the Prophet % is again a clear acknowledgement of his military prowess.

His intelligence and sound judgement is proved by the fact that he was one of the first to accept Islam.

He left for Paradise after gaining martyrdom having lived this earthly life for forty-one years.

“Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28)

Huthaifah bin Yaman The Prophet % said:

“O Huthaifah! You have the right to call

yourself either a Mohajir or an Ansar.

Huthaifah bin Yaman 2 was of medium height and was sturdily built. He had shining white teeth that seemed to glitter at times like diamonds. His eyes were as sharp and keen as those of a hawk, and he had no problem finding the target for his arrows even in the dark. He was an experienced and valiant general who led a temperate life. He was also the confidant of the Prophet 3. He had been the big-hearted and ascetic governor of Madyan. He used to keep awake at nights, praying. He knew the Noble Qur’4n by heart; in other words he was a Hafidh of the Noble Qur’an. He was also learned in the Hadith.

He was the fortunate Companion who by his farsighted caution and vital planning in the Battle of Ahzab, won the goodwill and confidence of the Prophet 3. He was the son of Haseel bin Jabr Al ‘Absi Al-Yamani 2 who had been martyred in the Battle of Uhud. He was an ally of the Ansér, and also helped the migrants.

Haseel bin Jabr was from the tribe of ‘Abbas which was known for its courage but had a reputation for treachery, murder and plunder as well. Haseel bin jabr had killed a man ! from his own tribe, and fled in panic to Al-Madinah. There he had found refuge with a man from the Banu ‘Abd Al-Shahal tribe. He was from Yemen and since the celebrated tribes of Aus and Khazraj were originally from Yemen, he had no

} Observance of tribal loyalty was an honourable tradition among the Arab tribes. Murder and plunder were common, in fact_ men who committed such acts against other tribes or wayfarers were considered brave heroes.

difficulty in finding a safe haven there. Very soon he got married to Rubab bin Ka‘b, a young woman from the Banu ‘Abd Al-Shahal tribe. Soon a son was born to them whom they named Huthaifah.

Since Haseel bin Jabr was originally from Yemen, the people of AlMadinah named _ the boy Yamani. He came to be known by this name and soon his original name was forgotten.

When the son of Amenah, Muhammad % proclaimed himself to be the Messenger of the One True Allah, Huthaifah and his father migrated to Makkah and pledged allegiance in person to the Prophet %, and entered the fold of Islam

Thus, these fortunate father and son had the honour of being the very first people to migrate in the cause of Islam. Since they were allies of the Banu ‘Abd Al-Shahal in Al-Madinah they were blessed in that they were able to help those who migrated there from Makkah. Thus they were Ansars as well. This is why the Prophet 3 said they had the liberty to call themselves either Ansar or Mohdjir; but they preferred to be known as Ansér.

Huthaifah bin Yaman # is one of the select band of Companions who gained the distinction of performing daring exploits in the battlefield. He displayed remarkable courage in all the wars fought by the Prophet #, side by side with him. The only battle he missed was the Battle of Badr. Even here, both father and son set out to join the army, but they were encircled by the Quraish. Abu Jahl asked Yamani ,

“Who are you young man and who is this old man with you? “ The youth replied very calmly;

“Tam Huthaifah bin Yaman ‘Abasi and this is my father, Haseel bin Jabr ‘Abasi.”

Abu Jahl said, “Oso you are going to help Muhammad 3%?” He replied, “We are going to Al-Madinah.”

Abu Jahl told them that he was in charge of them and if they tried to go anywhere they would be killed. One of Abu Jahl’s warriors then

whispered to him that if they harmed these two the whole ‘Abas tribe would turn against them and it would not be in their interests to rouse the animosity of this whole tribe. On hearing this, Abu Jahl shook his head and questioned,

“So you both wish to go to Al-Madinah? We will free you on one condition; swear that you will not help Muhammad in the Battle of Badr.”

The father and son took the oath in order to save their necks as there was no other way of getting out of the stranglehold he had on them. When the finally reached Al-Madinah, they told the Prophet % what had happened. He listened to them affectionately and then displaying great nobility of character, he said:

“You both should not break your promise to these disbelievers. Allah % is our Protector and Helper.”

Thus the Prophet %, by asking his Companions to remain true to their word given to an enemy made clear a principle which was fundamental and unique in the annals of military history. Courageous and great people always keep to their word of honour. In this instance it was the first battle of Islam; besides the unarmed Mujahideen numbered only three hundred and thirteen, whereas the enemy numbered one thousand fully armed soldiers. The need of the hour was to have as many experienced men as possible. It was truly a great decision taken by the Prophet 3 - to allow two of his best and most valued men to sit out only because the enemy had perforce extracted a promise from them.

Huthaifah bin Yaman 4: and his father always regretted the fact that they had lost this opportunity to take part in one of the greatest battles of Islam.

In the Battle of Badr Allah # blessed His small band of Mujahideen with an astounding success. The generals of the disbelievers were slain. The arrogant and egotistical Abu Jahl intoxicated by his power, met the most disgraceful end possible. There was mourning in most of the houses of the enemy, who were left helpless. But they did not give up for long; the thirst for revenge reached it peak in the year 3r¢ Hijrah. They attacked Al-Madinah under the Command of Abu Sufyan - to avenge the death of their kith and kin at Badr.

The Prophet 3# came out at the head of the Muslim army to defend the city and encamped at the foot of Mount Uhud. The warriors of the two armies faced each other in the battlefield. The disbelievers could not withstand the attack of the Mujahideen. Leaving behind all their arms and equipment they fled. The Mujahideen started to gather the booty of war. The Prophet 3 had left a band of fifty men under the leadership of ‘Abdullah bin Jubair 4, on the mountain, and instructed them not to move out of the mountain pass under any circumstances. Most of them thought this order was valid only for the duration of the battle; so they also left the mountain unguarded and joined the others in collecting the booty.
content_id:
Ketika pasukan kembali ke Al-Madinah setelah perang, sebagian penduduk kota tidak puas dengan kinerja mereka; sambil melemparkan tanah ke udara mereka berteriak, "Lihatlah! Para pengecut yang melarikan diri dari medan perang telah kembali!"

Ketika Nabi ﷺ melihat hal ini, beliau bersabda:

"Dengarlah, mereka bukanlah pengecut, dan mereka tidak melarikan diri dari medan perang. Ini adalah strategi militer - untuk memulihkan diri dan kembali menghadapi musuh."

Tidak diragukan lagi ini adalah langkah bijaksana dari Nabi ﷺ.

Menyelamatkan segelintir sahabat dari mulut naga, demikian ibaratnya, adalah bukti keberanian dan keteguhan hati yang luar biasa. Ini adalah eksploitasi militer pertama Khalid bin Waleed رضي الله عنه setelah masuk Islam. Seiring berjalannya waktu, ia menunjukkan kualitas kepahlawanan dan keberaniannya yang tak ternilai dalam kadar yang semakin besar.

Penyair istana Nabi ﷺ, Hassan bin Thabit رضي الله عنه, melantunkan secara spontan bait-bait syair ratapan yang indah namun menyayat hati, yang telah menjadi karya klasik penting dalam genre puisi elegis Arab. Ketika beliau syahid, Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه berusia sekitar empat puluh satu tahun.

Pada saat wafatnya beliau sedang melantunkan bait-bait syair epik perang; berikut ini adalah terjemahan kasarnya -

Alangkah agungnya! Kini dekat sudah Jannah yang suci dan airnya yang sejuk;

Bagi bangsa Romawi, siksa Jahannam sudah dekat.

Orang-orang kafir ini, sejak lahir jauh dari kemuliaan;

Adalah kewajibanku untuk memenggal kepala mereka saat aku melewati mereka.

Kaisar Najashi menerima Islam karena tergerak oleh ajakan Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه. 'Najashi' adalah gelar yang umum bagi semua penguasa Ethiopia. Nama penguasa yang sezaman dengan Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه adalah Ashamah. Tertulis dalam 'Tabaqat Ibn Sa'd' bahwa ketika Nabi ﷺ kembali ke Al-Madinah setelah menandatangani Perjanjian Hudaibiyah pada tahun 8 Hijriah, beliau mengutus 'Amr bin Umayyah Al-Dhamri ke Ethiopia kepada Kaisar Najashi dengan membawa surat. Beliau

menerima surat itu dan menciumnya dengan penuh hormat, turun dari singgasananya. Menunjukkan keramahan dan kerendahan hati yang penuh penghormatan, beliau duduk di tanah, dan mengucapkan sumpah setia kepada Islam.

Pada tahun ke-9 Hijriah saat beliau kembali dari Tabuk, beliau wafat. (Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un.)

Dalam sebuah hadith yang diriwayatkan dari Jabir رضي الله عنه dan tercatat dalam Sahih Al-Bukhari, disebutkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ mengumumkan wafatnya Najashi kepada para Sahabatnya dan menyalatkan jenazahnya.

Ibn Hibban meriwayatkan dari 'Imran bin Hussain bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda, "Saudara kalian Najashi telah wafat, marilah kita menyalatkan jenazahnya." Beliau maju ke depan dan para Sahabatnya membentuk barisan di belakangnya. Selama salat beliau mengucapkan "Allahu Akbar" empat kali. Sumpah setia kepada Islam oleh Najashi di hadapan Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه merupakan peristiwa bersejarah.

Keunggulan unik Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه dalam penampilan dan karakter memang patut dikagumi. Beliau memotong kaki kudanya sebelum syahid dan bertempur seolah dikuasai oleh hasratnya akan kesyahidan. Ini adalah pertanda penghargaan besar yang akan dianugerahkan kepadanya oleh Allah ﷻ.

Fakta bahwa beliau diangkat ke kedudukan yang tinggi di surga adalah bukti dan pengakuan atas jasa-jasa besar yang telah diberikannya kepada Islam. Penunjukannya

sebagai Panglima Tertinggi dalam Perang Mu'tah oleh Nabi ﷺ sekali lagi merupakan pengakuan jelas atas kehebatan militernya.

Kecerdasan dan penilaiannya yang tajam dibuktikan oleh fakta bahwa beliau termasuk orang-orang pertama yang menerima Islam.

Beliau berangkat menuju surga setelah meraih kesyahidan setelah menjalani kehidupan dunia selama empat puluh satu tahun.

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang ridha lagi diridhai-Nya!" (QS. Al-Fajr: 28)

Huthaifah bin Yaman Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Wahai Huthaifah! Engkau berhak menyebut

dirimu sebagai Muhajir atau Anshar."

Huthaifah bin Yaman رضي الله عنه bertubuh sedang dan tegap. Giginya putih bersinar yang terkadang tampak berkilauan bagai berlian. Matanya setajam dan sewaspada mata elang, dan beliau tidak kesulitan menemukan sasaran panahnya bahkan dalam kegelapan. Beliau adalah seorang jenderal yang berpengalaman dan pemberani yang menjalani kehidupan sederhana. Beliau juga merupakan orang kepercayaan Nabi ﷺ. Beliau pernah menjadi gubernur Madyan yang berjiwa besar dan zuhud. Beliau sering terjaga di malam hari untuk beribadah. Beliau hafal Al-Qur'an; dengan kata lain beliau adalah seorang hafidh Al-Qur'an. Beliau juga sangat mendalami ilmu hadith.

Beliau adalah Sahabat yang beruntung yang dengan kewaspadaan jauh pandangnya dan perencanaan vital dalam Perang Ahzab, memenangkan niat baik dan kepercayaan Nabi ﷺ. Beliau adalah putra Husail bin Jabr Al-'Absi Al-Yamani رضي الله عنه yang telah syahid dalam Perang Uhud. Beliau adalah sekutu kaum Anshar, dan juga membantu para muhajirin.

Husail bin Jabr berasal dari suku 'Abbas yang dikenal karena keberaniannya namun juga memiliki reputasi pengkhianatan, pembunuhan dan perampokan. Husail bin Jabr telah membunuh seorang laki-laki dari sukunya sendiri, dan melarikan diri dalam kepanikan ke Al-Madinah. Di sana ia menemukan perlindungan pada seorang dari suku Banu 'Abd Al-Shahal. Ia berasal dari Yaman dan karena suku-suku terkenal Aus dan Khazraj pada asalnya berasal dari Yaman, ia tidak

Ketaatan pada loyalitas suku adalah tradisi yang terhormat di kalangan suku-suku Arab. Pembunuhan dan perampokan adalah hal yang umum, bahkan orang-orang yang melakukan perbuatan tersebut terhadap suku lain atau musafir dianggap sebagai pahlawan yang berani.

kesulitan menemukan tempat perlindungan yang aman di sana. Tidak lama kemudian ia menikah dengan Rubab binti Ka'b, seorang wanita muda dari suku Banu 'Abd Al-Shahal. Segera seorang putra lahir bagi mereka yang mereka namakan Huthaifah.

Karena Husail bin Jabr pada asalnya berasal dari Yaman, penduduk Al-Madinah menamai anak itu Yamani. Ia dikenal dengan nama ini dan segera nama aslinya terlupakan.

Ketika putra Aminah, Muhammad ﷺ menyatakan dirinya sebagai Utusan Allah Yang Maha Esa, Huthaifah dan ayahnya berhijrah ke Makkah dan berbaiat secara langsung kepada Nabi ﷺ, dan memeluk Islam.

Dengan demikian, ayah dan anak yang beruntung ini mendapat kehormatan menjadi orang-orang pertama yang berhijrah demi Islam. Karena mereka adalah sekutu Banu 'Abd Al-Shahal di Al-Madinah, mereka diberkahi sehingga dapat membantu mereka yang berhijrah ke sana dari Makkah. Dengan demikian mereka juga termasuk Anshar. Inilah sebabnya Nabi ﷺ bersabda bahwa mereka bebas menyebut diri mereka Anshar atau Muhajir; namun mereka lebih memilih dikenal sebagai Anshar.

Huthaifah bin Yaman رضي الله عنه termasuk dalam kelompok terpilih dari para Sahabat yang meraih keistimewaan melakukan eksploitasi berani di medan perang. Beliau menunjukkan keberanian luar biasa dalam semua peperangan yang diperjuangkan Nabi ﷺ, berdampingan dengan beliau. Satu-satunya perang yang tidak diikutinya adalah Perang Badar. Bahkan di sini pun, ayah dan anak sama-sama berangkat untuk bergabung dengan pasukan, tetapi mereka dikepung oleh kaum Quraisy. Abu Jahl bertanya kepada Yamani,

"Siapa kamu wahai anak muda dan siapa orang tua yang bersamamu ini?" Pemuda itu menjawab dengan sangat tenang;

"Aku Huthaifah bin Yaman 'Abasi dan ini ayahku, Husail bin Jabr 'Abasi."

Abu Jahl berkata, "Jadi kalian akan membantu Muhammad ﷺ?" Ia menjawab, "Kami hendak pergi ke Al-Madinah."

Abu Jahl memberitahu mereka bahwa ia bertanggung jawab atas mereka dan jika mereka mencoba pergi ke mana pun mereka akan dibunuh. Salah seorang prajurit Abu Jahl kemudian

berbisik kepadanya bahwa jika mereka menyakiti keduanya, seluruh suku 'Abbas akan berbalik melawan mereka dan tidak menguntungkan bagi mereka untuk membangkitkan permusuhan seluruh suku ini. Mendengar ini, Abu Jahl menggelengkan kepalanya dan bertanya,

"Jadi kalian berdua ingin pergi ke Al-Madinah? Kami akan membebaskan kalian dengan satu syarat; bersumpahlah bahwa kalian tidak akan membantu Muhammad dalam Perang Badar."

Ayah dan anak itu mengambil sumpah demi menyelamatkan nyawa mereka karena tidak ada jalan lain untuk keluar dari cengkeraman yang mengikat mereka. Ketika akhirnya mereka tiba di Al-Madinah, mereka menceritakan kepada Nabi ﷺ apa yang telah terjadi. Beliau mendengarkan mereka dengan penuh kasih sayang dan kemudian menunjukkan kemuliaan akhlak yang agung, beliau bersabda:

"Kalian berdua jangan melanggar janji kalian kepada orang-orang kafir itu. Allah ﷻ adalah Pelindung dan Penolong kita."

Dengan demikian Nabi ﷺ, dengan meminta para Sahabatnya untuk tetap setia pada janji yang diberikan kepada musuh, telah menegaskan suatu prinsip yang mendasar dan unik dalam catatan sejarah militer. Orang-orang yang pemberani dan agung selalu menepati janji kehormatan mereka. Dalam peristiwa ini, itu adalah peperangan pertama Islam; di samping itu para Mujahidin yang tidak bersenjata hanya berjumlah tiga ratus tiga belas orang, sedangkan musuh berjumlah seribu prajurit bersenjata lengkap. Kebutuhan saat itu adalah memiliki sebanyak mungkin orang berpengalaman. Itu benar-benar keputusan besar yang diambil oleh Nabi ﷺ - mengizinkan dua orang terbaik dan paling berharga dari para Sahabatnya untuk tidak ikut berperang hanya karena musuh telah memaksa mengambil janji dari mereka.

Huthaifah bin Yaman 4: dan ayahnya selalu menyesali kenyataan bahwa mereka telah kehilangan kesempatan untuk ikut serta dalam salah satu pertempuran terbesar dalam Islam.

Dalam Perang Badr, Allah # memberkahi pasukan kecil mujahidin-Nya dengan kemenangan yang menakjubkan. Para jenderal kaum kafir terbunuh. Abu Jahl yang sombong dan angkuh, yang mabuk oleh kekuasaannya, menemui akhir yang paling memalukan. Duka cita melanda sebagian besar rumah musuh, yang dibuat tak berdaya. Namun mereka tidak menyerah untuk waktu yang lama; kehausan akan balas dendam mencapai puncaknya pada tahun ke-3 Hijrah. Mereka menyerang Al-Madinah di bawah komando Abu Sufyan - untuk membalas kematian kerabat mereka di Badr.

Nabi 3# keluar memimpin pasukan Muslim untuk mempertahankan kota dan berkemah di kaki Gunung Uhud. Para pejuang kedua pasukan saling berhadapan di medan perang. Kaum kafir tidak mampu menahan serangan para mujahidin. Meninggalkan semua senjata dan perlengkapan mereka, mereka melarikan diri. Para mujahidin mulai mengumpulkan harta rampasan perang. Nabi 3 telah menempatkan sekelompok lima puluh orang di bawah pimpinan 'Abdullah bin Jubair 4, di atas gunung, dan memerintahkan mereka untuk tidak meninggalkan celah gunung dalam keadaan apa pun. Sebagian besar dari mereka mengira perintah ini hanya berlaku selama pertempuran berlangsung; maka mereka pun meninggalkan gunung tanpa penjagaan dan bergabung dengan yang lain mengumpulkan harta rampasan.
content_ar: عندما the الجيش returned to Al-المدينة المنورة بعد the war, some of the people of the city were not happy with their performance; tossing up earth in the air they shouted, “Look! The cowards who fled from the المعركةfield have come back!” عندما النبي ﷺ # saw this قال: “Listen, these people are not cowards, neither have they run away from the المعركةfield. This is a military strategy - to refresh themselves and return to confront the العدو. ” Doubtless this was a wise move by النبي ﷺ 38. To get a handful of الصحابة out of the mouth of the dragon, as it were, is proof of great bravery and الشجاعة. This was the أولاً martial exploit of Khalid bin Waleed 4 بعد he converted to الإسلام. As time passed he displayed his invaluable qualities of heroism and valour in ever greater measure. The nightingale of the court of النبي ﷺ %, Hassan bin Thabit &, recited extempore beautifully sad الآيات, which have become المهم classics of the elegiac genre of Arabic poetry. عندما he was الشهيدed Ja’far bin Abi Talib - was about forty-one years old. At the time of الموت he was reciting the الآيات of a war epic; the following is a rough translation - This is great! Near now the pure Jannah and its cool waters; For the Romans the torture of Jahannum is near. These unbelievers, by birth are far from nobility; It is my الواجب to lop off their heads as I pass them. Emperor Najashi accepted الإسلام لأن he was moved by the invitation of Ja’far bin Abi Talib 4. ‘Najashi’ was a title common to all tulers of Ethiopia. The name of the ruler who was a contemporary of Ja’far bin Abi Talib 4 was Ashamah. It is written in the ‘Tabagat Ibn Sa‘d' that عندما النبي ﷺ 3% returned to Al-المدينة المنورة بعد signing the Treaty of Hudaibiyah in the year 8 Hijrah, he sent ‘Amr bin Umayyah Al-Dhamri to Ethiopia to Emperor Najashi with a letter. He received the letter and kissed it reverently, descending from his throne. Displaying respectful الضيافة and التواضع, he sat down on the ground, and recited the oath of allegiance to الإسلام. In the year 9'b Hijrah on his return from Tabook, he passed away. (To الله we belong and to Him we return.) In a التراث attributed to Jabir # and recorded in صحيح البخاري قيل that النبي ﷺ # announced the passing away of Najashi to his الصحابة and offered his funeral الصلوات. Ibn Habban has narrated on the authority of ‘Imran bin Hassain that النبي ﷺ 3 said, “Your الأخ Najashi has expired, let us offer funeral الصلوات for him.” He moved forward and his الصحابة formed rows behind him. خلال the الصلوات he recited “Alla@ho Akbar’ four times. The swearing of the pledge of allegiance to الإسلام by Najashi in the presence of Ja’far bin Abi Talib .& is a historic incident. The unique excellence of Ja’far bin Abi Talib & in appearance and الأخلاق was indeed enviable. He cut off the legs of his horse قبل he was الشهيدed and قاتل as إذا obsessed by his passion for الشهيدdom. This was the forerunner of great honours to be bestowed on him by الله ¥%. The fact that he was elevated to a lofty position in الجنة is proof and acknowledgement of the great services rendered by him to الإسلام. His appointment as القائد-in-Chief in the المعركة of Motah by النبي ﷺ % is again a clear acknowledgement of his military prowess. His intelligence and sound judgement is proved by the fact that he was one of the أولاً to accept الإسلام. He left for الجنة بعد gaining الشهيدdom having lived this earthly الحياة for forty-one years. “Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28) Huthaifah bin Yaman النبي ﷺ % said: “O Huthaifah! You have the الحق to call yourself either a Mohajir or an Ansar. Huthaifah bin Yaman 2 was of medium height and was sturdily built. He had shining white teeth that seemed to glitter at times like diamonds. His eyes were as sharp and keen as those of a hawk, and he had no problem finding the target for his arrows even in the dark. He was an experienced and valiant general who led a temperate الحياة. He was أيضاً the confidant of النبي ﷺ 3. He had been the big-hearted and ascetic governor of Madyan. He used to keep awake at nights, praying. He knew the Noble Qur’4n by القلب; بعبارة أخرى he was a Hafidh of the Noble Qur’an. He was أيضاً learned in the الحديث. He was the fortunate Companion who by his farsighted caution and الجوهري planning in the المعركة of Ahzab, won the goodwill and confidence of النبي ﷺ 3. He was the son of Haseel bin Jabr Al ‘Absi Al-Yamani 2 who had been الشهيدed in the المعركة of Uhud. He was an ally of the Ansér, and أيضاً helped the migrants. Haseel bin Jabr was from the tribe of ‘Abbas which was known for its الشجاعة but had a reputation for treachery, murder and plunder as well. Haseel bin jabr had killed a man ! from his own tribe, and fled in panic to Al-المدينة المنورة. There he had found refuge with a man from the Banu ‘Abd Al-Shahal tribe. He was from Yemen and بما أن the celebrated tribes of Aus and Khazraj were originally from Yemen, he had no } Observance of tribal loyalty was an honourable التراث among the Arab tribes. Murder and plunder were common, in fact_ men who committed such acts against other tribes or wayfarers were considered brave heroes. difficulty in finding a safe haven there. Very soon he got married to Rubab bin Ka‘b, a young woman from the Banu ‘Abd Al-Shahal tribe. Soon a son was born to them whom they named Huthaifah. بما أن Haseel bin Jabr was originally from Yemen, the people of AlMadinah named _ the boy Yamani. He came to be known by this name and soon his original name wأماgotten. عندما the son of Amenah, محمد ﷺ % proclaimed himself to be الرسول ﷺ of the One True الله, Huthaifah and his الأب migrated to مكة المكرمة and pledged allegiance in person to النبي ﷺ %, and entered the fold of الإسلام وهكذا, these fortunate الأب and son had the honour of being the very أولاً people to migrate in the cause of الإسلام. بما أن they were allies of the Banu ‘Abd Al-Shahal in Al-المدينة المنورة they were blessed in that they were able to help those who migrated there from مكة المكرمة. وهكذا they were Ansars as well. This is why النبي ﷺ 3 said they had the liberty to call themselves either Ansar or Mohdjir; but they preferred to be known as Ansér. Huthaifah bin Yaman # is one of the select band of الصحابة who gained the distinction of performing daring exploits in the المعركةfield. He displayed remarkable الشجاعة in all the wars قاتل by النبي ﷺ #, side by side with him. The only المعركة he missed was the المعركة of Badr. Even here, both الأب and son set out to join the الجيش, but they were encircled by the Quraish. Abu Jahl asked Yamani , “Who are you young man and who is this old man with you? “ The youth replied very calmly; “Tam Huthaifah bin Yaman ‘Abasi and this is my الأب, Haseel bin Jabr ‘Abasi.” Abu Jahl said, “Oso you are going to help محمد ﷺ 3%?” أجاب, “We are going to Al-المدينة المنورة.” Abu Jahl told them that he was in charge of them and إذا they tried to go anywhere they would be killed. One of Abu Jahl’s warriors then whispered to him that إذا they harmed these two the whole ‘Abas tribe would turn against them and it would not be in their interests to rouse the animosity of this whole tribe. On hearing this, Abu Jahl shook his head and questioned, “لذا you both wish to go to Al-المدينة المنورة? We will free you on one condition; swear that you will not help محمد ﷺ in the المعركة of Badr.” The الأب and son took the oath من أجل save their necks as there was no other الطريق of getting out of the stranglehold he had on them. عندما the وأخيراً reached Al-المدينة المنورة, they told النبي ﷺ % what had happened. He listened to them affectionately and then displaying great nobility of الأخلاق, قال: “You both should not break your promise to these disbelievers. الله % is our Protector and Helper.” وهكذا النبي ﷺ %, by asking his الصحابة to remain true to their word given to an العدو made clear a principle which was الأساسي and unique in the annals of military history. Courageous and great people always keep to their word of honour. In this instance it was the أولاً المعركة of الإسلام; بالإضافة إلى the unarmed المجاهدين numbered only three hundred and thirteen, في حين the العدو numbered one thousand fully armed soldiers. The need of the hour was to have as many experienced men as possible. It was truly a great decision taken by النبي ﷺ 3 - to allow two of his best and most valued men to sit out only لأن the العدو had perforce extracted a promise from them. Huthaifah bin Yaman 4: and his الأب always regretted the fact that they had lost this opportunity to take part in one of the greatest المعركةs of الإسلام. In the المعركة of Badr الله # blessed His small band of المجاهدين with an astounding الفلاح. The generals of the disbelievers were slain. The arrogant and egotistical Abu Jahl intoxicated by his power, met the most disgraceful end possible. There was mourning in most of the houses of the العدو, who were left helpless. But they did not give up for long; the thirst for revenge reached it peak in the year 3r¢ Hijrah. They attacked Al-المدينة المنورة under the Command of Abu Sufyan - to avenge the الموت of their kith and kin at Badr. النبي ﷺ 3# came out at the head of the مسلم الجيش to defend the city and encamped at the foot of Mount Uhud. The warriors of the two armies faced بعضهم بعضاً in the المعركةfield. The disbelievers could not withstand the attack of the المجاهدين. Leaving behind all their arms and equipment they fled. The المجاهدين started to gather the booty of war. النبي ﷺ 3 had left a band of fifty men under the leadership of ‘Abdullah bin Jubair 4, on the mountain, and instructed them not to move out of the mountain pass under any circumstances. Most of them thought this order was valid only for the duration of the المعركة; لذا they أيضاً left the mountain unguarded and joined the others in collecting the booty.
